content.full_text:
RAvI MEI-ITA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
October 30, 1996
Ray Johnson, Councilmember
City of Los Altos Hills
13090 Alta Lane
Los Altos Hills, CA 94022-3401
Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. 1-96-260
Dear Mr. Johnson:
This is in response to your request for advice under the campaign disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’ Since you are merely seeking guidance regarding the Act’s
reporting provisions, your letter is considered a request for informal assistance.
2
QUESTION
Are you required to report as a contribution the services provided by the League of
Women Voters and its eight partners in the Smart Voter Project, which will provide a free web
site on the Internet for your committee at no charge?
CONCLUSION
No. The League of Women Voters and its partners are providing a forum on the Internet
for all candidates on the San Mateo and Santa Clara County November ballots. Forums provided
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California
Code of Regulations, Section 18000-18995. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an
opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)
42 J S
iui. 1. Si
it S()O, S
ii io. C,\uFolNI 95S 14
(9 I 6) 322—56O


Advice File No. 1-96-260
Page 3
The League of Women Voters and its partners formed a nonpartisan web site and invited
all candidates on the ballot in San Mateo and Santa Clara Counties to participate. In addition to
providing a mechanism for candidates to communicate to voters, the project provides voters
direct access to information about election contests occurring in their precincts.
While
trditional forums” occur in a meeting place with a live audience, this nonpartisan web site
made available to all candidates in the two-county area meets the exception to the definition of a
“contribution.”
Accordingly, since the forum provided by the Smart Voter Project is not a contribution
under the Act, you have no obligation to report the value of the use of Smart Voter Project Web
site.
I trust this answers your question sufficiently. If you have additional questions, or need
to discuss this further, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
9eff1 Cof

By:
Jeanette B. Turvil
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
SGC/JET/jt
